A.text: along the outer surface of each backbone

B.text: only at the start and end of each backbone

C.text: on one side of a backbone, without connecting to the opposite backbone

parsed.rationale: The image clearly shows the DNA double helix structure with two sugar-phosphate backbones forming the outer sides. The nitrogen bases (colored rectangles) are depicted connecting the two backbones, positioned in the space between them. This arrangement is consistent with the standard biological model of DNA where base pairs form the rungs of the ladder-like structure. Therefore, the nitrogen base pairs are located between the two backbones.
